channel.title: prometheus on Oleksandr Kulbida
channel.description: Recent content in prometheus on Oleksandr Kulbida

item.title: PromQL cheat sheet
item.description: Full visual reference: https://y0zg.github.io/public-blog/pf-infographic-promql-cheatsheet.pdf Key functions rate vs irate rate calculates per-second increase averaged over the whole range. Use it for alerts and graphs — it’s smoother. irate only looks at the last two data points. Very responsive but noisy. Avoid for alerting. rate(http_requests_total[5m]) irate(http_requests_total[5m]) increase — total increase over the range. Only use for display, not for alerts. Use rate for alerts. increase(process_cpu_seconds_total[15m]) histogram_quantile — must apply rate first, then aggregate buckets, then quantile.
